content: Management of NTI drugs requires: therapeutic drug monitoring (TDM) with timing critical (trough levels before next dose; peak levels when indicated); consistent formulation use (brand vs generic switching may alter levels); patient education on adherence and interaction recognition; regular clinical assessment for toxicity signs. Warfarin: monitor INR weekly initially, then monthly when stable; maintain food consistency (vitamin K). Digoxin: check level 6-8 hours post-dose; monitor renal function, potassium, magnesium. Lithium: check level 12 hours post-dose; monitor thyroid, renal function; maintain sodium/fluid intake. Phenytoin: monitor free levels in hypoalbuminemia; watch for nystagmus, ataxia, confusion. Gentamicin: extended-interval dosing with random levels or traditional dosing with peak/trough.
